text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Breast Oncology Program

ATTENDING ONCOLOGIST: [Attending]
FELLOW: [Fellow]

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Ms. Okafor is a 49-year-old West African woman with Stage IIB (T2N1M0) triple-negative breast cancer who presents for oncology follow-up at the conclusion of adjuvant capecitabine and pembrolizumab. She is here today primarily to review her completed treatment course, discuss residual toxicities, and plan next steps including potential adjuvant olaparib.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Okafor was originally diagnosed with a 3.2 cm left breast upper outer quadrant invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3, ER/PR/HER2-negative, Ki-67 85%, with one sentinel lymph node positive on initial biopsy. NGS confirmed a germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT) along with somatic TP53 p.R248W, PIK3CA p.H1047R, RB1 splice-site mutation, PTEN p.R130Q, MYC amplification, and CDKN2A homozygous deletion. HRD score was 68, gLOH 22%, PD-L1 CPS 18. She received neoadjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg Q3W plus dose-dense AC x4 cycles (ddAC Q2W with pegfilgrastim) followed by weekly paclitaxel 80 mg/m2 x12 weeks with concurrent pembrolizumab per the KEYNOTE-522 backbone. Her neoadjuvant course was notable for two immune-related events: grade 2 autoimmune thyroiditis (thyrotoxic phase transitioning to hypothyroidism, now on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily) and grade 2 sensory peripheral neuropathy requiring paclitaxel dose reduction to 80% from week 7 onward, managed with duloxetine 30 mg daily.

Post-neoadjuvant breast MRI demonstrated partial radiographic response with a residual 1.6 cm enhancing mass and persistent left axillary cortical thickening. She subsequently underwent left modified radical mastectomy with ALND (levels I-II) and right prophylactic mastectomy with bilateral immediate tissue expander reconstruction. Surgical pathology confirmed non-pCR: residual IDC 1.8 cm (ypT1c), 3 of 16 left axillary nodes positive (ypN1a), LVI present, and RCB Index 2.8 (RCB Class III — extensive residual disease). The right prophylactic mastectomy showed only benign fibrocystic changes.

Given RCB Class III residual disease and three persistently positive nodes after neoadjuvant therapy, she received post-mastectomy radiation therapy to the left chest wall and regional lymph nodes (total dose 60 Gy to chest wall including boost, 50 Gy to regional nodes), completed over approximately 6.5 weeks, concurrent with adjuvant capecitabine. PMRT was complicated by grade 2 radiation dermatitis with moist desquamation in the axillary fold, which has since resolved.

Adjuvant capecitabine was started at 1500 mg BID (1000 mg/m2 BID based on BSA 1.73 m2) on a 14-days-on/7-days-off schedule Q21 days, concurrent with continuing pembrolizumab to complete her planned approximately 1 year of immunotherapy (17 total cycles inclusive of neoadjuvant). She developed grade 2 palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia (hand-foot syndrome) during capecitabine Cycle 3, requiring a dose reduction to 75% (1125 mg BID), which brought the HFS down to grade 1. All 8 planned cycles of capecitabine were completed.

During adjuvant pembrolizumab Cycle 14, she was found to have asymptomatic grade 3 immune-related hepatitis on routine LFT monitoring (ALT 412 U/L, AST 389 U/L, bilirubin normal). Pembrolizumab was permanently discontinued at that point per standard management guidelines. She was treated with methylprednisolone 1 mg/kg IV x3 days followed by prednisone 80 mg daily PO (1 mg/kg) with a slow taper over 8 weeks. Hepatology was consulted; workup for viral hepatitis and autoimmune hepatitis serologies was negative. Capecitabine was continued uninterrupted through the hepatitis episode. LFTs have since normalized. She is currently on prednisone 20 mg daily with ongoing taper.

She completed her final (8th) cycle of capecitabine approximately 3 weeks ago. She presents today for reassessment and discussion of olaparib adjuvant therapy.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- General: Denies fever, chills, significant weight loss. Reports mild fatigue, improved from nadir.
- Neurologic: Persistent bilateral hand and foot tingling/numbness, manageable, no pain, not interfering with daily activities. Denies new focal weakness.
- GI: Mild residual nausea on some days, improved. No diarrhea, no abdominal pain. Appetite adequate. No jaundice.
- Skin/extremities: HFS resolved. No new rash. Radiation dermatitis at left axillary fold healed with residual hyperpigmentation only.
- Endocrine: No heat/cold intolerance. No palpitations since initial thyroiditis episode. Taking levothyroxine as prescribed.
- Musculoskeletal: No new bone pain or arthralgias.
- Respiratory: No dyspnea or cough.
- Hematologic: No easy bruising, no bleeding. Mild exertional fatigue possibly consistent with residual anemia from recent chemotherapy.
- Reproductive: Last menstrual period occurred approximately 4 months after chemotherapy completion; currently irregular cycles. No hot flashes.
- Psychological: Anxiety related to cancer recurrence concern, well-managed. Support from family.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY (updated):
1. Stage IIB TNBC, left breast — as above
2. Germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant — enrolled in genetics registry, gynecologic oncology referral pending
3. Immune-mediated thyroiditis (pembrolizumab-related) — on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily
4. Immune-related hepatitis grade 3 (pembrolizumab-related) — resolved on steroid taper
5. Chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy grade 1 — on duloxetine 30 mg daily
6. Hand-foot syndrome grade 2 (capecitabine-related) — resolved after dose reduction
7. Chemotherapy-induced transient amenorrhea — now menstruating irregularly

FAMILY HISTORY:
Mother: ovarian cancer, age 52. Maternal aunt: breast cancer, age 44. Consistent with hereditary BRCA2-associated cancer syndrome. Genetic counseling has been provided; family members have been offered testing.

SOCIAL HISTORY:
High school biology teacher, currently on reduced workload but plans to return to full-time. Non-smoker, occasional alcohol prior to diagnosis (now abstains). No illicit drug use. Married with two adult children. Strong support system.

ALLERGIES:
No known drug allergies.

MEDICATIONS (current):
1. Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO daily (thyroid replacement, immune-mediated hypothyroidism)
2. Duloxetine 30 mg PO daily (peripheral neuropathy)
3. Prednisone 20 mg PO daily (tapering — for immune-mediated hepatitis; currently on taper schedule)
4. Calcium 600 mg + Vitamin D 1000 IU BID (bone protection while on steroids)
5. Omeprazole 20 mg PO daily (GI protection while on steroids)
6. Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN nausea
7. Multivitamin

PHYSICAL EXAM:
Vitals: BP 118/72 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, RR 16, Temp 36.9°C, SpO2 98% on room air, Weight 72.1 kg, BMI 27.3.
General: Well-appearing woman in no acute distress, pleasant, engaged in discussion.
HEENT: Sclerae non-icteric, mucous membranes moist, no oral lesions.
Neck: Supple. No palpable cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. No thyroid enlargement or tenderness.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No peripheral edema.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes or crackles.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly. Bowel sounds normal.
Breast/Chest wall: Bilateral mastectomy scars well-healed. Tissue expanders in place bilaterally; no overlying skin changes, no erythema, no firmness suggestive of infection. Left chest wall post-radiation skin with mild residual hyperpigmentation; healed, no moist desquamation. No palpable chest wall nodules.
Axilla: Left axilla with expected post-ALND changes, no palpable masses or adenopathy. Right axilla unremarkable.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves intact. Reduced sensation to light touch in bilateral stocking-glove distribution (hands up to wrists, feet up to mid-lower leg), symmetric. Normal strength in all extremities. Normal coordination.
Skin: No rash. No palmer/plantar erythema.
Extremities: No lymphedema appreciated clinically. Full range of motion bilateral upper and lower extremities.
ECOG Performance Status: 0.

LABORATORY RESULTS (obtained today):
CBC: WBC 6.2 x10^9/L, Hgb 11.3 g/dL (baseline pre-treatment 13.1 g/dL), Plt 244 x10^9/L, MCV 98 fL. Mild anemia, macrocytic, likely persistent chemo effect and/or steroid-related; trending upward from nadir.
Comprehensive Metabolic Panel: Na 138, K 4.1, Cr 0.8 mg/dL. Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL. ALT 28 U/L (reference <40), AST 24 U/L (reference <40). Alkaline phosphatase 72 U/L. Albumin 3.9 g/dL. LFTs fully normalized — excellent hepatic recovery.
TSH: 1.8 mIU/L (WNL on current levothyroxine dose).
Blood glucose: 112 mg/dL (mildly elevated, likely steroid-related; monitoring recommended).
HgbA1c: 5.7% (borderline, steroid effect — no intervention required yet, will recheck after steroid discontinuation).
Fasting lipid panel: deferred to PCP.

IMAGING:
Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (obtained approximately 2 weeks prior): No evidence of local recurrence or distant metastatic disease. Left chest wall post-mastectomy and radiation changes stable. Tissue expanders in place. No hepatic lesions. No pulmonary nodules. No suspicious adenopathy or osseous lesions. Consistent with no evidence of disease.

ASSESSMENT:
Ms. Okafor is a 49-year-old woman with Stage IIB TNBC (germline BRCA2, TP53/PIK3CA/RB1 co-mutated, HRD-high), RCB Class III residual disease after neoadjuvant pembrolizumab + ddAC/paclitaxel, who has now completed adjuvant capecitabine (8 cycles, with dose reduction to 75% for HFS) and 14 cycles of pembrolizumab (permanently discontinued for grade 3 immune hepatitis). She is clinically well with ECOG PS 0 and no evidence of disease on recent cross-sectional imaging.

Key issues today:
1. Treatment completion — all planned adjuvant therapies (cape, pembro) have been administered.
2. Residual toxicities — grade 1 peripheral neuropathy (improving), resolving immune hepatitis (LFTs normal), mild steroid-related hyperglycemia, mildly macrocytic anemia (improving).
3. Adjuvant olaparib consideration — patient has germline BRCA2 and high-risk residual disease (RCB Class III, node-positive), making her an ideal candidate per the OlympiA regimen data.
4. Prednisone taper ongoing — needs to complete before starting olaparib to minimize overlapping toxicities (nausea, fatigue, anemia).
5. BRCA2 germline management — gynecologic oncology referral for RRBSO discussion is outstanding; patient is premenopausal with irregular cycles; this is an important long-term risk-reduction priority.
6. Tissue expander exchange — plastic surgery coordination for permanent implant exchange when medically cleared.

PLAN:

1. ADJUVANT OLAPARIB (OlympiA regimen):
Ms. Okafor meets high-risk eligibility criteria per OlympiA trial: germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant, TNBC histology, HER2-negative, residual disease at surgery (non-pCR with RCB Class III), and completion of all standard neoadjuvant and adjuvant chemotherapy. The OlympiA data demonstrated a statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvement in invasive disease-free survival (approximately 8-9% absolute benefit at 3 years) and overall survival benefit in this population, with the greatest magnitude of benefit in the node-positive/RCB high-risk group. Reviewed the data in detail with the patient, including discussion of risks (anemia, nausea, fatigue, potential for MDS/AML — rare with short-duration adjuvant use).
- Plan to start olaparib 300 mg BID after prednisone taper is complete and LFTs have remained normal for at least 2-3 additional weeks (estimated start approximately 2-3 weeks from today).
- Baseline CBC and LFTs to be rechecked at time of olaparib initiation.
- Will monitor CBC monthly for the first 3 months, then every 6-8 weeks thereafter.
- Planned duration: 52 weeks (1 year).
- Patient verbalized understanding and is eager to proceed. She has reviewed educational materials from the nurse navigator.

2. STEROID TAPER (prednisone for immune-mediated hepatitis):
- Currently at prednisone 20 mg daily.
- Plan to continue taper: reduce by 5 mg every 2 weeks: 15 mg x 2 weeks, then 10 mg x 2 weeks, then 5 mg x 2 weeks, then discontinue.
- Total estimated taper completion: approximately 6 weeks from today.
- LFTs to be rechecked in 3 weeks while tapering; if any recrudescence of transaminase elevation, will hold taper and reassess.
- Calcium/Vit D and omeprazole to continue while on steroids, can discontinue after steroid completion.
- Monitor blood glucose during ongoing steroid taper; if fasting glucose rises above 126 or patient becomes symptomatic, will refer to PCP for management.

3. PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY:
- Grade 1, stable to improving. Duloxetine 30 mg daily to be continued.
- No dose escalation planned at this time. Will reassess at next visit.
- Patient reminded to avoid injury to extremities, use non-slip mats, check water temperature.

4. THYROID FUNCTION:
- TSH 1.8 mIU/L — excellent on levothyroxine 75 mcg. Continue current dose.
- Recheck TSH in approximately 6 months.

5. ANEMIA:
- Mild (Hgb 11.3 g/dL), trending upward from nadir. Likely multifactorial: post-chemotherapy, possible iron deficiency component, steroid-related. No intervention required now.
- Will recheck CBC in 3 weeks at next visit. Iron studies to be obtained at next check.

6. BRCA2 GERMLINE MANAGEMENT — GYNECOLOGIC ONCOLOGY:
- Referral to gynecologic oncology for discussion of risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRBSO) is outstanding. Given germline BRCA2 and irregular menses (possible chemo-induced ovarian insufficiency), RRBSO is strongly recommended once she has recovered from current treatment course and steroid taper is complete.
- Patient aware and agrees with this priority. Family counseling for first-degree relatives reinforced.

7. BREAST RECONSTRUCTION:
- Bilateral tissue expanders in place. Coordination with plastic surgery for exchange to permanent implants. Timing to be determined in consultation with plastic surgery; generally deferred until patient medically stable and steroid taper complete. Target: after olaparib initiation and initial tolerance established.

8. SURVEILLANCE:
- Next CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: scheduled at 12 months post-surgery (approximately 6 months from now).
- Ongoing semi-annual clinical follow-up.
- Annual mammography not indicated given bilateral mastectomy; MRI of bilateral implants can be considered annually per implant surveillance protocol.

9. SUPPORTIVE CARE / ANCILLARY:
- Oncology nurse navigator to reach out regarding olaparib start logistics and prescription assistance.
- Social work referral offered; patient declines at this time.
- Patient encouraged to maintain physical activity — supervised exercise program referral provided.
- Return to oncology in 3 weeks for steroid taper check, repeat labs, and pre-olaparib assessment.

Time spent in counseling and coordination of care: approximately 50 minutes.

Electronic signature: [Attending Oncologist, MD]
Reviewed and co-signed by: [Fellow, MD]
